subject: Product Management
title: PM vs CEO
description: A short lesson to help you apply PM vs CEO.

hook.text: People call the PM 'the CEO of the product.' Most actual CEOs find that comparison laughable.

buildup.text: CEOs have authority: they hire, fire, set budgets, and make final calls. PMs have none of that.

discovery.text: The comparison captures one truth: PMs must think holistically — users, business, technology, strategy.

twist.text: But calling PMs 'mini-CEOs' creates dangerous expectations. PMs who act like CEOs get fired.

climax.text: A better title: 'the product's chief advocate.' Responsible for its success, accountable without authority.

punchline.text: All the responsibility of a CEO. None of the power.

quiz.question: Why is 'CEO of the product' a misleading title for PMs?
options: PMs have responsibility but no direct authority over people or budgets
PMs actually have more power than CEOs
CEOs don't think about products at all
